question_text: What is the pKa of a sp hybridized carbon atom? A) 25 B) 35 C) 45 D) 50

A) Choose the letter of the molecule with the lowest barrier to rotation (or ring-flip). B) Choose the letter of the molecule with the highest barrier to rotation (or ring-flip). A) ethane B) propane C) butane D) cyclohexane
Rank the following substituents in order of priority (1 = highest priority): -CH=CH2, -CN, -CH2NH2, -CH2Br

If a chiral molecule has an absolute configuration of R, which direction does it rotate the plane of polarized light? A) clockwise (dextrorotary) B) counterclockwise (levarotatory) C) it doesn’t rotate the plane of polarized light D) can’t be determined from the information given

A) Enter the pKa value for each acid in the boxes below. B) Indicate whether the reactants or products will be favored at equilibrium by choosing the appropriate set of equilibrium arrows (a longer arrow is drawn toward the species favored at equilibrium). The equilibria are: 1) Phenol + H-O⁻ ⇌ phenolate anion + H2O, 2) Acetate ion + protonated methylamine ⇌ acetic acid + methylamine. pKa values should be entered in boxes under each acid and appropriate equilibrium arrows chosen.

error_comment: Answer requires drawing chemical structures, electron lone pairs, and curved arrows, which is not text-oriented

question_text: Rank the following sets of molecules in order of acidity (1 = most acidic). First set: Protonated methylamine (H⁺-N(CH3)2), protonated methyl mercaptan (H⁺-S-CH3), protonated methylphosphine (H⁺-P(CH3)2), protonated chlorine (H⁺-Cl), protonated tert-butyl (H⁺-C(CH3)3). Second set: Protonated thioacetic acid (CH3C(=O)SH⁺), protonated methyl thioester (CH3C(=O)SCH3⁺), protonated acetamide (CH3C(=O)NH2H⁺), protonated acetone (CH3C(=O)CH3H⁺), protonated acetic acid (CH3C(=O)OH⁺). For each molecule, the answer should correspond to its rank order of acidity using placeholders {box_1} to {box_5} under each structure in sequence in both sets.

Met-enkephalin, an endorphin, serves as a natural pain reliever that changes or removes the perception of nerve signals. Label all of the functional groups present in Met-enkephalin. The structure shows multiple functional groups such as amine, phenol, amide, arene, carboxylic acid, and sulfide. Identify and label these groups in the given molecular structure.
canonical_answer: amine; phenol; amide; arene; carboxylic acid; sulfide

question_text: Name the following molecules. First molecule is an alkane with substituents including 3-methyl, 4-isopropyl, 5-methyl, and 6-methyl substituents on an octane chain. Second molecule is a bromocycloheptane with a tert-butyl substituent. Provide the correct IUPAC names for both.

error_comment: Task requires drawing and is not text-oriented

question_text: A) Circle the structures that represent a conformation of 2,2-dimethylbutane sighting along any C-C bond. (Five Newman projections shown: one with Me and H substituents scrabbled, one with Me and Et substituents, one with tBu and H substituents, and others as shown). B) Complete each of the Newman projections below to show the most stable and least stable conformations of 2,2-dimethylbutane, sighting along the C2-C3 bond. (Two partially completed Newman projections showing substituents with some handwritten notes on energies and eclipsing interactions.) C) Use the above Newman projections to calculate the barrier to rotation of 2,2-methylbutane sighting along the C2-C3 bond.
canonical_answer: A) Structures circled are the Newman projection with Me-H substituents arranged with Et and the Newman projection with tBu and H substituents circled; B) Answer not explicitly given in text form in image; C) 3.6 kcal/mol

question_text: A) Draw in the substituents on the ring flipped conformers of each molecule (A and B) to predict which molecule is lower in energy. B) Show the Newman projection for each ring-flipped conformer, sighting along the C5-C6 and C3-C2 bonds. C) Choose the chair conformation that is lower in energy for each molecule. D) Which structure is lower in energy, choose one: A) or B). E) Briefly explain your choice.
canonical_answer: C) A; D) A; E) A has fewer 1,3-diaxial interactions in its lowest energy conformation

question_text: A) Label each molecule as chiral (C), achiral (A), or achiral/meso (M). B) Designate each chirality center as R or S. Three molecules are shown: bicyclic with CH3 substituent, an amino acid derivative with Br and OH substituents and chiral centers marked, and a tetra-substituted carbon with OH and CH3 groups on a cyclohexane ring.
canonical_answer: A) A; C; M B) S-front, R-back

question_text: Indicate the relationships between the two molecules as enantiomers (E), diastereomers (D), or same molecule (S). Four pairs of molecules are shown: 1) cis-1,2-cyclopentanediol pair, 2) substituted cyclohexane pair with Br and CH2OH groups, 3) Br-CH3 substituted alkane with OH and CH2CH3 substituents, 4) Br and OH substituted alkane showing stereochemistry.

(4 pts) One of the steps in fat biosynthesis is the hydration of crotonate to yield 3-hydroxybutyrate. The reaction occurs by addition of -OH to the si face at C3, followed by protonation at C2, also from the si face. a) Label the top face of each alkene carbon atom as re or si. b) Show the stereochemistry of the product and c) label C3 as R or S. The question includes the reaction scheme showing crotonate (H3C-CH=CH-CO2-) converting to 3-hydroxybutyrate with OH and H additions, with annotations on carbons 2 and 3 and stereochemical labels re and si provided in the scheme.
canonical_answer: a) C3 = re face, C2 = si face; b) The product is (S)-3-hydroxybutyrate with hydroxyl group oriented accordingly; c) C3 is labeled as S
